executive_summary: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager is under active zero-day attack (CVE-2026-20245), allowing threat actors to gain root access; Google warns it was exploited for months before disclosure. The ShinyHunters cybercrime group breached Madison Square Garden, exposing 26 million records including facial recognition dossiers. Multiple ransomware groups (Akira, Qilin) continue to target organizations across sectors, and a sophisticated phishing kit called Bluekit is using browser-in-the-middle techniques to bypass MFA and steal AWS credentials in real-time. Ukraine's national postal service Ukrposhta was hacked overnight, and LastPass disclosed yet another data breach affecting user accounts.

items.description: Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager vulnerability allowing unauthenticated remote root access
items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: High

canada_landscape.summary: No direct Canadian cybersecurity incidents or CCCS advisories were identified in this 48-hour period. Canadian infrastructure and organizations should prioritize patching Cisco SD-WAN vulnerabilities given the active zero-day exploitation. The Canada-specific content primarily involved non-security news items.

report_2w.executive_summary: Canada's cyber agency CCCS issued an urgent advisory on FortiBleed, a mass credential leak affecting thousands of Fortinet firewall devices—credentials for these enterprise network perimeters are now circulating in attacker hands. Separately, a supply-chain breach at market intelligence firm Klue has cascaded into data breaches at multiple major cybersecurity vendors including Huntress, HackerOne, Jamf, Recorded Future, and Tanium via stolen OAuth tokens that accessed their Salesforce CRM data. Canada's spy service CSIS also used a first-of-its-kind legal warrant to remotely clean malware from infected devices inside the country. Healthcare breaches continue to dominate: Xsolis exposed 1.4 million patient records, and Texas Parks and Wildlife confirmed 3 million license holders affected.

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-014 warning of the FortiBleed mass credential leak impacting Fortinet devices. Canada's intelligence service CSIS used a first-of-its-kind legal warrant to remotely remove malware from infected devices within Canadian borders. The Gentlemen ransomware group listed Canada Wide Media as a victim, and London Hydro (a Canadian utility) disclosed a data breach exposing customer information.
